briefdescription.para: The entry point for showing the XCan driver in polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: The example configures the device for internal loopback mode, then sends a Can frame, receives the same Can frame, and verifies the frame contents.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, otherwise driver-specific error code.

- In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
If the device is not working correctly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the driver instance
XST_SUCCESS if successful, a driver-specific return code if not.
This function waits until TX FIFO has room for at least one frame before sending a frame. So this function may block if the hardware is not built correctly.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a frame and verifies its contents.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the driver instance
XST_SUCCESS if successful, a driver-specific return code if not.
This function waits until RX FIFO becomes not empty before reading a frame from it. So this function may block if the hardware is not built correctly.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the example.

detaileddescription.para: This function is not included if the example is generated from the TestAppGen test tool.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: Contains an example of how to use the XCan driver directly.

detaileddescription.para: The example here shows using the driver/device in polled mode.

The Baud Rate Prescaler Register (BRPR) and Bit Timing Register (BTR) are setup such that CAN baud rate equals 40Kbps, assuming that the the CAN clock frequency is 24MHz. The user needs to modify these values based on the desired baud rate and the CAN clock frequency. For more information see the CAN 2.0A, CAN 2.0B, ISO 11898-1 specifications.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 04/12/05 First release 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated driver to use the HAL APIs/macros. The macros have been renamed to remove _m from the name. 3.2 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 3.3 ask 08/01/18 Fixed Cppcheck and GCC warnings in can driver 3.7 ht 07/04/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.11 sp 21/07/25 Fix GCC Warnings. 3.12 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
